title: **Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026**
content:
DATA CHECK: The source table lists figures in thousands ($,000), but the narrative requires conversion to millions for consistency. All figures below are converted from the source's $,000 unit to a consistent million display.

The most significant balance-sheet development this period was growth in loans and leases. Total assets stood at 241.6 million as of March 31, 2026, increased 2.6%.

Loans and leases grew to 182.8 million, up 1.5%, reflecting a larger base of earning assets alongside the added exposure to credit risk. Investment securities declined to 33.1 million, down 3.6%. Cash and deposits fell to 12.7 million, down 46.7%.

Loans and leases is now the largest component of the balance sheet. That growth on the asset side was funded primarily through liabilities, reflecting how the institution's holdings were paid for during the period.

Total liabilities stood at 209.8 million, increased 3.6%, led by accounts payable, accrued interest on borrowings, and other liabilities at 5.0 million, down 46.2%. Accrued dividends & interest payable on shares & deposits remained zero over the period.

Total equity stood at 14.6 million, decreased 4.1%., supported by undivided earnings of 19.3 million and declining reserves. Taken together, the institution's retained capital base shrank while its lending footprint expanded during this quarter. Management continues to watch the pace of loan growth relative to deposit contraction.
